figures.caption: Table 1. Success Rates (%) on the LIBERO benchmark. Bold indicates the best performance and underline indicates the second best. Green/Red numbers in parentheses show the improvement/degradation over our baseline. Results for prior methods are taken from their original papers and are provided for reference only; implementation and citation details are provided in Appendix C.1.

Table 2. Success Rates (%) on the RoboTwin 2.0 benchmark. Bold indicates the best performance and underline indicates the second best. Green/Red numbers in parentheses show the improvement/degradation over the baseline. Results for prior methods are taken from RoboTwin2.0 leaderboard and are provided for reference only; implementation and citation details are provided in Appendix C.2.
Table 21. LA-Cond results on pick-and-place tasks.
Table 22. LA-Tok results on pick-and-place tasks.
Figure 11. Visualizations of pick-and-place task.
Figure 1. Overview of latent actions in VLA. Left: a unified VLA pipeline; right: our two perspectives and four integration strategies.

Figure 4. Real-world manipulation task results. Scores are reported on a [0,100] completion-percentage scale, details provided in Appendix F.2. Mean scores for each of the four real-world tasks across different models (10 rollouts per model-task), detailed results are in Appendix F.3.

Table 3. Real-world pick-and-place task results. Scores are reported on a [0,100] scale, details provided in Appendix F.2 and Appendix F.3. Bold indicates the best performance and underline indicates the second best. Green/Red numbers in parentheses show the improvement/degradation over the baseline in the average columns.
Table 13. Baseline results on stacking and wiping tasks.
Table 15. LA-Direct results on stacking and wiping tasks.
Table 14. LA-Align results on stacking and wiping tasks.
Table 11. Single-task training vs. multi-task joint training on 10 RoboTwin2.0 tasks. Single denotes per-task training, and Multi denotes joint training on all tasks.
Table 16. LA-Cond results on stacking and wiping tasks.
Table 18. Baseline results on pick-and-place tasks.
Table 17. LA-Tok results on stacking and wiping tasks.
Figure 7. Architecture of the fine-tuned image-based latent action model.

Figure 3. Architectural instantiations of the Baseline and four VLM supervision strategies. (a) Baseline. (b) Strategy 1 (Implicit Representation Alignment): LA-Align. (c) Strategy 2 (Explicit Direct Decoding): LA-Direct. (d) Strategy 3 (Explicit Conditional Decoding): LA-Cond. (e) Strategy 4 (Action-to-Token Mapping): LA-Tok.
Figure 8. Learning curves on LIBERO-Spatial (top left), LIBERO-Object (top right), LIBERO-Goal (bottom left), LIBERO-Long (bottom right).
